quotes: H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
H2 (section heading): 22px / 30px (≈136%), Nunito Sans Bold
H3 (subhead): 18px / 26px (≈144%), Nunito Sans Bold or SemiBold
Secondary/supporting body: 14px / 21px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular

relations.note: Campaign headline exception narrows the general Agenda/Nunito Sans hierarchy for digital use.
Both are GENERAL email typography baseline rules governing font delivery and scale together.
Line-height minimum rule elaborates the body/footnote line-heights already set in the type scale.

quotes: **Agenda** (Adobe font)
**Nunito Sans**
Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.
Available Nunito Sans weights: Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold.
Available Aptos weights: Regular, Bold.
Headlines & headers | **Agenda** (Adobe font) | **Nunito Sans** (Google font), bolder weights, Title Case
Body copy | **Nunito Sans** | **Nunito Sans** | **Aptos**
Footers, fine print | Nunito Sans | Nunito Sans | Aptos
Available Agenda weights: Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold.

rules.intent: Preserve the locked cross-medium typeface and weight hierarchy for headlines, body, and footers.
rules.rule_text: Headlines/headers use Agenda in print, Nunito Sans (bolder weights, Title Case) in digital/email, and Aptos in Word/PPT/Excel; body copy and footers/fine print use Nunito Sans in print and digital, Aptos in Word. Font weight usage is restricted to the available sets: Agenda — Light, Regular, Medium, Semibold, Bold; Nunito Sans — Light, Regular, SemiBold, Bold, ExtraBold; Aptos — Regular, Bold.

effect.subject: Agenda (font.headline.print = Agenda) used as live text in digital

quotes: Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only
*Agenda may be used as live text in digital for the campaign headline only*

rules.intent: Reserve the print headline typeface as a digital exception exclusively for the campaign headline lockup.
rules.rule_text: Agenda (font.headline.print, campaign_headline.font default = Agenda) may only be used as live text in digital contexts for the campaign headline — never for other digital headlines or body text, which remain Nunito Sans.

quotes: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
Nunito Sans must be loaded via `@font-face`/Google Fonts link where the client supports it; the stack guarantees graceful fallback in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

rules.intent: Ensure consistent brand typography rendering with safe fallback across email clients.
rules.rule_text: Email CSS font-family must use the fallback stack ('Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif); Nunito Sans must be loaded via @font-face/Google Fonts where supported, so the stack guarantees graceful degradation in Outlook and other clients that strip webfonts.

quotes: Body: 16px / 24px (150%), Nunito Sans Regular
Footnote/reference text within a section: 12px / 18px (150%)
H1 (section hero headline): 28px / 36px (≈130%), Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold
16px is the brand-mandated minimum for body copy ("bigger is better")
use sparingly, never for primary content
must be "noticeably smaller" than primary data and clearly separated from it
Never set running text below 12px.

rules.intent: Preserve a legible, consistent size/weight hierarchy across email section levels.
rules.rule_text: Email type scale runs H1 28px/36px (Nunito Sans ExtraBold or Bold) → H2 22px/30px (Bold) → H3 18px/26px (Bold or SemiBold) → Body 16px/24px (Regular, brand-mandated minimum for body copy) → Secondary/supporting 14px/21px (Regular, used sparingly, never for primary content) → Footnote 12px/18px, which must be noticeably smaller than and clearly separated from primary data. Running text must never be set below 12px (ISI sizing is owned by its locked component).

quotes: Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy
Line height: minimum 150% for all body copy** (brand accessibility rule). **For light text on dark backgrounds, increase further** — [GENERAL] use 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

rules.intent: Guarantee minimum readability/accessibility of body text, with extra spacing on dark backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: All body copy requires a minimum line height of 150% (brand accessibility rule); for light text on dark backgrounds, increase further to 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

quotes: Use a clear **type scale / visual hierarchy** so the reader knows what to read first, next, last. Never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight to signal different hierarchy levels.

rules.intent: Prevent ambiguous reading order caused by visually indistinguishable adjacent text levels.
rules.rule_text: Maintain a clear type scale/visual hierarchy so the reader knows what to read first, next, and last; never use two adjacent text blocks at the same size+weight combination to signal different hierarchy levels.
